response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine language-tooling interests (built a D test runner, assertion library, and worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments), but the core stack for this role is largely absent: no demonstrated Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise Java framework experience, and only minor Java exposure. Recent work is TypeScript/Node full-stack and AI tooling rather than JVM ecosystems, making the fit for an enterprise Java/Kotlin IDE role weak despite strong craftsmanship credentials.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) plus self-built developer tooling (test runner, assertion library)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, teaching and workshops
Proven ability to work across low-latency, high-scale systems (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only peripheral Java exposure; JVM depth not demonstrated
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise framework / application server background
Recent focus (TypeScript/Node, AI agents) diverges from enterprise JVM tooling domain

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling experience
